consent: If you consent to participate, please select the first option to begin the study.
consent_2:
IMPORTANT:



Please complete the survey in one sitting, stay on-task, and avoid distractions. 



We have recently noticed an increase in participants starting the task and then idling while doing other things. To measure off-task behavior, we use a mouse-tracking tool that can detect if you leave the task. This tool logs the time you spend on- and off-task during the survey. If this tool identifies that you spent more than 15 minutes off-task, your submission will be rejected. To protect your privacy, we do not record any other information about your browsing activity.



There will also be screening and attention checks during this survey, and your submission may be rejected if any are failed. If you complete this survey more than once, you will not be eligible for the study, and you will not be paid for your participation.



Finally, we use Prolific and Qualtrics bot-detection tools to identify automated or bot-generated responses as well as use of generative AI tools, such as chatGPT. If your responses are flagged as likely generated by a bot or AI, your submission will be rejected. We are interested in learning about your thoughts, feelings, and experiences.

 
 -----------------------------------------------------------------------------


Please check the box below to acknowledge that your submission will be rejected and you will not receive payment if:  


	you spend more than 15 minutes off-task; 
	you fail screening and attention checks;
	your submission is flagged as likely generated by a bot or AI by Prolific and Qualtrics.

research_3b: Academic research for me has helped in various ways. From gaining extra credit through attending research studies, to performing research for labs, and helping me discover helpful information about myself. To begin, I have gained extra credit through participation in studies at my university. I would gain points in my classes by completing the surveys, interviews, or observations. I also performed research myself in my psychology labs. Performing this research helped me to understand its importance and how to properly and ethically conduct experiments. Finally, I have gained knowledge that has helped my personal life through the studies conducted at my school. For example, I learned the proper amount of sleep I should be getting each day by attending a research lab regarding performance levels after certain hours of sleep. I learned that after about seven and a half hours of sleep I perform at the best rate. After I implemented this into my life I felt better all around and I was so grateful. Academic research has helped me in various ways. I have educated myself and helped keep myself safer by getting the proper amount of sleep.
govtfund_1b: I approve of the use of federal government using taxpayer funds to invest in research at colleges to a certain extent. research_3b: The positive effects that academic research has had on me are helping me to understand that many of the things I think or feel are not in my head, and I am not making them up. Before participating in academic research, I could never truly find the information I was looking for online because I did not know how to put it into words on what I was experiencing or how I was feeling. There have been so many papers and videos I have read on different topics that are relevant to me, and it just made me feel like I was not alone in my thinking. If academic research was being done on something, then there were other experiences like mine. The negative effects are that I just cannot get any help for my issues. Academic research is being conducted, but there are not many opportunities to get help.
